title: tripsacum muticum
intro: Tripsacum muticum, also known as Eastern Broom Sedge, is a perennial grass native to eastern North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to soothe and protect the skin from infections and wounds. The health benefits of Tripsacum muticum include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems such as coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
